Set up a Colab environment with Python 3.10.12 and install TensorFlow and Keras 3.2.1
Import TensorFlow as tf and import keras_nlp from keras_nlp module
Import random module for sampling test sentences

Load or clone the KerasNLP English-to-Spanish translation example notebook from https://colab.research.google.com/github/keras-team/keras-io/blob/master/examples/nlp/ipynb/neural_machine_translation_with_keras_nlp.ipynb
Run all cells up to the model training section to successfully train the translation model
Define the decode_sequences function as in the example, ensuring it calls keras_nlp.samplers.GreedySampler() with arguments including end_token_id set to the Spanish tokenizer's end token id
Prepare a test set of English sentences (e.g., test_eng_texts) with at least 2 sample sentences to translate
Invoke decode_sequences on a batch containing one English sentence, e.g., decode_sequences([test_eng_texts[0]])
Observe that the function call to GreedySampler.__call__() includes the unexpected keyword argument 'end_token_id'
Verify that a TypeError is raised with the message: "Sampler.__call__() got an unexpected keyword argument 'end_token_id'"
Confirm the traceback points to the line where GreedySampler() is called inside decode_sequences
Repeat the call with a second sample sentence from test_eng_texts to confirm reproducibility
Assert that the error consistently occurs in the decode_sequences function when calling keras_nlp.samplers.GreedySampler() with end_token_id argument
